title: Vagrant: Verify Vagrant Box Catalog spec compliance
description: Verify Vagrant handler against Vagrant box catalog format. Spec: https://developer.hashicorp.com/vagrant/docs/boxes/format. Must support: vagrant box add from custom URL. Test with native vagrant client. Document any gaps.

title: Conda: Verify Conda Channel spec compliance
description: Verify CondaNative handler against Conda channel format. Spec: https://conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/user-guide/concepts/channels.html. Must support: conda install -c <repo>, repodata.json generation. Test with native conda/mamba client. Document any gaps.
close_reason: Conda channel handler implemented and tested

title: Debian/APT: GPG-Signed Release & Full Metadata
description:
Make Debian/APT handler production-ready:
- Sign Release file to produce InRelease (inline GPG) and Release.gpg (detached)
- Serve GPG public key at /debian/{repo}/gpg endpoint for client import
- Add Packages.xz (xz-compressed index, preferred by modern apt)
- Add Contents-{arch}.gz (file listing index)
- Add Acquire-By-Hash support (serve index files by hash for CDN compatibility)
- Add proper Release fields: Origin, Label, Codename, Suite, Components, Architectures, Date, Valid-Until
- Support for multiple components (main, contrib, etc.) and distributions (stable, bullseye, etc.)
Spec: https://wiki.debian.org/DebianRepository/Format
close_reason: Signing integration implemented and tested

title: CRAN: Verify CRAN Repository spec compliance
description: Verify CRAN handler against CRAN repository format. Spec: https://cran.r-project.org/doc/manuals/R-admin.html#Setting-up-a-package-repository. Must support: install.packages() from custom repo. Test with native R client. Document any gaps.
close_reason: Closed

title: Puppet: Verify Puppet Forge API compliance
description: Verify Puppet handler against Puppet Forge API v3. Spec: https://forgeapi.puppet.com/. Must support: puppet module install, puppet module publish. Test with native puppet client. Document any gaps.
close_reason: Closed

title: SBT/Ivy: Verify Ivy Repository layout compliance
description: Verify SBT handler against Ivy repository layout. Spec: https://ant.apache.org/ivy/history/latest-milestone/concept.html. Must support: sbt publish, sbt update with custom resolver. Test with native sbt client. Document any gaps.
close_reason: Closed

title: Swift/SPM: Verify Swift Package Registry spec compliance
description: Verify Swift handler against SE-0292 Swift Package Registry. Spec: https://github.com/swiftlang/swift-package-manager/blob/main/Documentation/PackageRegistry/Registry.md. Must support: swift package resolve from registry. Test with native swift client. Document any gaps.
close_reason: Closed

title: Full-text search engine (Meilisearch/Manticore)
description: Replace basic SQL ILIKE/tsvector search with a dedicated search engine for comprehensive artifact discovery. Current issues: (1) Site search page is broken - frontend calls /search/quick, /search/advanced, /search/checksum but backend has /search/artifacts, /search/suggest, /search/trending, /search/recent. (2) No cross-entity search (repos + artifacts + metadata in one query). (3) No faceted navigation, relevance ranking, or typo tolerance. Options: Meilisearch (lightweight, Rust-based), Manticore Search (MySQL-compatible), or Elasticsearch. Scope: add search container to docker-compose, index sync service, unified search API, and rebuild the frontend Search page.

title: Security & Artifact Scanning
description: Comprehensive security scanning system for artifacts and container images. Includes: vulnerability scanning for packages (CVE detection), container image scanning (layer analysis, base image vulnerabilities), repository security scores (aggregate scoring from scan results), scan policy enforcement (block downloads/deployments based on severity thresholds), scan dashboard with findings drill-down. The sidebar already has a Security nav group with a 'Security Scans' Coming Soon placeholder (from nav refactor epic t52). This epic implements the actual scanning backend and frontend. Builds on existing artifact metadata, format handlers, and the centralized AppError pattern.
close_reason: Security scanner epic complete: dependency scanning, Trivy image scanning, scan-on-upload, policy enforcement, and integration tests

title: Vulnerability Database & Scanner Engine
description: Core scanning engine that checks artifact dependencies against known vulnerabilities. Backend: Add vulnerability_db table (cve_id, package_name, affected_versions, severity, description, published_at, source). Add scan_results table (id, artifact_id, repository_id, scan_type enum[dependency|image|license|malware], status enum[pending|running|completed|failed], started_at, completed_at). Add scan_findings table (id, scan_result_id, finding_type, severity enum[critical|high|medium|low|info], title, description, cve_id, affected_component, affected_version, fixed_version, source_url). Implement ScannerService with trait for pluggable scan backends. Initial implementation parses package manifests (package.json, Cargo.toml, requirements.txt, pom.xml) and checks against vulnerability_db. Files: backend/src/models/scan.rs, backend/src/services/scanner_service.rs, backend/src/services/scanners/dependency_scanner.rs, backend/migrations/
close_reason: Implemented scanner_service.rs with DependencyScanner, AdvisoryClient (OSV.dev + GitHub Advisory), trait-based Scanner interface, manifest parsing (npm, cargo, pip, go, maven, ruby, nuget), and score recalculation orchestration

title: Container Image Scanning
description: Scan Docker/OCI container images for vulnerabilities in OS packages and application dependencies. Parse image manifests and layer contents. Extract package lists from common base images (alpine apk, debian apt, redhat rpm). Cross-reference with vulnerability database. Store layer-level findings. Integrate with the scanner trait from issue 1. Files: backend/src/services/scanners/image_scanner.rs, backend/src/formats/docker.rs (extend existing)
close_reason: Implemented Trivy image scanner, scan-on-upload, and security integration tests

title: Repository Security Scores
description: Aggregate scan findings into per-repository security scores. Score calculation: weighted average based on finding severity (critical=10, high=7, medium=4, low=1) normalized to 0-100 scale (100=no issues). Add repo_security_scores table (id, repository_id, score, critical_count, high_count, medium_count, low_count, last_calculated_at). Recalculate on each scan completion. Add API endpoint GET /api/v1/repositories/:key/security-score. Show score badge on repository list and detail pages. Files: backend/src/services/security_score_service.rs, backend/src/models/security_score.rs, backend/migrations/, backend/src/api/handlers/repositories.rs
close_reason: Implemented repo_security_scores table, recalculate_score(), get_score(), get_all_scores(), get_dashboard_summary() in scan_result_service.rs, Grade enum with A-F scoring (90-100=A, 75-89=B, 50-74=C, 25-49=D, 0-24=F), penalty weights (critical=25, high=10, medium=3, low=1)

title: Scan API Endpoints
description: REST API for triggering scans and retrieving results. Endpoints: POST /api/v1/security/scans (trigger scan for artifact/repo), GET /api/v1/security/scans (list scans with filters), GET /api/v1/security/scans/:id (scan detail with findings), GET /api/v1/security/scans/:id/findings (paginated findings), GET /api/v1/security/summary (org-wide summary stats), POST /api/v1/security/policies (create scan policy), GET /api/v1/security/policies (list policies). All endpoints require auth. Use existing DTO patterns from api/dto.rs. Files: backend/src/api/handlers/security.rs, backend/src/api/routes.rs
close_reason: Implemented all security API endpoints in handlers/security.rs: GET /dashboard, GET /scores, POST /scan, GET/GET:id scans, GET findings, POST/DELETE acknowledge, full CRUD policies, repo-scoped security config. Registered in routes.rs.

title: Scan Policy Enforcement
description: Define and enforce security policies that gate artifact downloads and deployments. Add scan_policies table (id, name, repository_id nullable for global, max_severity enum[critical|high|medium|low], block_unfixed bool, block_unscanned bool, is_enabled). Enforcement points: artifact download handler checks policy before serving, returns 403 with policy violation details. Admin can create global policies (all repos) or per-repo policies. Files: backend/src/models/scan_policy.rs, backend/src/services/policy_service.rs, backend/src/api/handlers/security.rs, backend/src/formats/ (add policy check to download paths)
close_reason: Implemented policy_service.rs with evaluate_artifact() checking block_unscanned, block_on_fail, max_severity threshold. CRUD for scan_policies. Integrated into API endpoints.

title: Security Dashboard Frontend
description: Frontend page for the Security nav group. Replace the Coming Soon placeholder with a full security dashboard. Page sections: (1) Summary cards - total scans, critical findings, repos at risk, policy violations. (2) Recent scans table with status, findings count, severity breakdown. (3) Top vulnerabilities list (most common CVEs across repos). (4) Repository score leaderboard (worst-to-best). (5) Scan trigger button (select artifact/repo to scan). (6) Findings detail drawer with CVE info, affected versions, fix recommendations. Add scan policy management page. Files: frontend/src/pages/SecurityScans.tsx, frontend/src/pages/SecurityPolicies.tsx, frontend/src/components/security/ScanResultsTable.tsx, frontend/src/components/security/FindingsDrawer.tsx, frontend/src/components/security/ScoreCard.tsx, frontend/src/api/security.ts, frontend/src/App.tsx
close_reason: Implemented SecurityDashboard.tsx (summary cards, score leaderboard), SecurityScans.tsx (scan list with filters, trigger modal), SecurityScanDetail.tsx (findings table with acknowledge/revoke, CVE links, expandable rows), SecurityPolicies.tsx (full CRUD). Updated Sidebar.tsx with live links, App.tsx with routes, api/security.ts, types/security.ts. Updated E2E tests.

title: Automatic Scan on Upload
description: Automatically trigger vulnerability scans when artifacts are uploaded. Add scan_on_upload flag to repositories table (default false). When enabled, artifact upload handler queues a scan job after successful storage. Use tokio::spawn for async scanning so uploads are not blocked. Add webhook event artifact_scanned. Update scan results in background. Files: backend/src/api/handlers/artifacts.rs, backend/src/services/scanner_service.rs, backend/src/models/repository.rs, backend/migrations/
close_reason: Implemented Trivy image scanner, scan-on-upload, and security integration tests

title: Security Integration Tests
description: Integration tests for the scanning system. Test: (1) dependency scan finds known CVE in test package.json, (2) image scan detects vulnerable base image layer, (3) repo score recalculates after scan, (4) policy blocks download of artifact with critical finding, (5) auto-scan triggers on upload when enabled. Files: backend/tests/security_tests.rs, scripts/native-tests/test-security-scan.sh
close_reason: Implemented Trivy image scanner, scan-on-upload, and security integration tests

title: Format Spec Compliance: Native Protocol Support for All Package Types
description: Systematically verify and implement native client protocol support (push + pull/download) for all 49 supported package formats, following each format's official specification. Each format should work like Docker V2 — native clients (pip, npm, cargo, gem, etc.) can push and pull directly without custom tooling. Document the spec source for each format.

title: Helm: Verify Helm Chart Repository spec compliance
description: Verify Helm handler against Helm Chart Repository spec. Spec: https://helm.sh/docs/topics/chart_repository/ and OCI-based charts via OCI Distribution Spec. Must support: helm repo add, helm install, helm push. Test with native helm client. Document any gaps.
close_reason: Helm Chart Repository handler implemented and tested

title: Bazel: Verify Bazel Registry spec compliance
description: Verify Bazel handler against Bazel Central Registry protocol. Spec: https://bazel.build/external/registry. Must support: bazel build with custom registry. Test with native bazel client. Document any gaps.

title: Alpine/APK: RSA-Signed APKINDEX & Full Metadata
description:
Make Alpine/APK handler production-ready:
- RSA-sign APKINDEX: include .SIGN.RSA.{keyname}.pub file in APKINDEX.tar.gz
- Serve RSA public key at /alpine/{repo}/{branch}/keys/{keyname}.rsa.pub
- Add DESCRIPTION file per repository
- Parse actual APK package headers on upload to extract real metadata (dependencies, provides, install_if, etc.) rather than relying on filename parsing
- Support multiple branches (edge, v3.19, v3.18, etc.) and repositories (main, community, testing)
- Validate APK package format on upload
Spec: https://wiki.alpinelinux.org/wiki/Apk_spec
close_reason: Signing integration implemented and tested

title: GitLFS: Verify Git LFS Batch API compliance

close_reason: Closed

title: Format Expansion - 50+ Package Types

New native formats needed: Alpine APK, Conda, PHP Composer, Hex, CocoaPods, Chef, Puppet, Chocolatey, Swift, CRAN, Git LFS, Terraform, sbt, Vagrant, Opkg, P2 Eclipse, Pub/Dart, AI Editor Extensions. Also add specialized ML/AI repository support for Hugging Face and ML models.

title: GPG Key Management System for Repository Signing
description: Add GPG key management infrastructure: generate/import/store RSA/GPG signing keys per repository. Store keys securely (encrypted at rest). Provide API endpoints for key creation, rotation, public key export. Support both RSA (Alpine) and GPG (Debian/RPM) key types. Admin UI for key management. This is the foundation for all three system package signing features.

title: Borg Replication - Mesh Edge Sync with Priority Policies & Resilient Transfer
description: Evolve the existing hub-spoke edge node system into a mesh topology where any edge can replicate to/from any other edge. Features: (1) Repository priority policies (P0=always replicated, P1=scheduled, P2=on-demand), (2) Resumable chunked artifact transfers for degraded networks, (3) Mesh peer discovery and edge-to-edge replication, (4) Network-aware scheduling with bandwidth controls. Builds on existing infrastructure: edge_nodes table, sync_tasks, EdgeService, edge/ binary with LRU cache and heartbeat loop.
close_reason: Closed

title: Repo Replication Priority Policies
description: Add replication priority policies to repositories. Add replication_priority column (P0/P1/P2/P3) to repositories table via migration. P0=always replicated to all assigned edges immediately, P1=replicated on configurable schedule (cron), P2=replicated on-demand when first requested, P3=never replicated (local-only). Add priority field to EdgeRepoAssignment to allow per-edge overrides. Update EdgeService to filter sync tasks by priority policy. Backend: migration, model updates, service logic. Files: backend/migrations/, backend/src/models/repository.rs, backend/src/models/edge_node.rs, backend/src/services/edge_service.rs, backend/src/api/handlers/edge.rs
close_reason: All Wave 1 work complete: 4 migrations (028-031), models (ReplicationPriority enum, transfer_session, peer_connection, edge_node network fields), services (transfer_service, peer_service, edge_service updates, repository_service updates), handlers (transfer, peer, edge updates), routes wired, docs (README, swarm-protocol, priority-policies). Compilation passes.

title: Resumable Chunked Transfer Protocol
description: Implement chunked artifact transfer with resume support for degraded networks. Split artifacts into configurable chunks (default 1MB). Backend: Add transfer_sessions table (id, sync_task_id, total_chunks, completed_chunks, chunk_size, checksum_algo, created_at). Add endpoints: POST /api/v1/edge-nodes/:id/transfer/init (returns session + chunk manifest), GET /api/v1/edge-nodes/:id/transfer/:session/chunk/:n (download chunk), POST /api/v1/edge-nodes/:id/transfer/:session/verify (verify checksums). Edge: Update fetch_from_primary to use chunked downloads, track progress per chunk, resume from last completed chunk on reconnect. Add per-chunk SHA256 verification. Files: backend/src/models/transfer_session.rs, backend/src/services/transfer_service.rs, backend/src/api/handlers/transfer.rs, backend/migrations/, edge/src/sync.rs, edge/src/transfer.rs
close_reason: Implemented chunked transfer client in edge binary: transfer.rs with init_transfer, chunk manifest, multi-source download with retry, SHA-256 verification, bitmap availability updates, scored peer selection. sync.rs updated with fetch_artifact_by_id that auto-selects chunked vs simple based on size. Resume support via manifest status check.

title: Mesh Peer Discovery
description: Enable edge nodes to discover and register each other as replication peers. Add peer_connections table (id, source_node_id, target_node_id, status, latency_ms, bandwidth_estimate_bps, last_probed_at). Edge nodes announce themselves to the central registry which maintains the peer graph. Add endpoints: GET /api/v1/edge-nodes/:id/peers (list known peers), POST /api/v1/edge-nodes/:id/peers/probe (trigger latency/bandwidth probe to another node). Edge binary: Add peer discovery loop that queries central for nearby nodes (same region first, then cross-region). Store peer list locally for offline operation. Files: backend/src/models/peer_connection.rs, backend/src/services/peer_service.rs, backend/src/api/handlers/edge.rs, backend/migrations/, edge/src/peers.rs, edge/src/main.rs
close_reason: Implemented mesh peer discovery in edge binary: peers.rs with discover_peers, probe_peer_latency, report_probe_result, report_peer_unreachable, and background peer_discovery_loop (60s interval). Integrated into main.rs as background task. Probes peers for latency/bandwidth and reports results to primary registry.

title: Edge-to-Edge Replication
description: Allow edge nodes to replicate artifacts directly from peer edges instead of always pulling from central. When an edge needs an artifact: (1) check local cache, (2) check peer edges (prefer lowest latency peer that has it), (3) fall back to central. Add replication_source field to sync_tasks (central|peer). Edge binary: Add artifact serving endpoint so peers can pull from each other. Add peer cache index exchange (lightweight bloom filter or hash set of cached artifact IDs, exchanged on peer probe). Central tracks which edges have which artifacts via cache entries for routing decisions. Files: edge/src/replication.rs, edge/src/peers.rs, edge/src/sync.rs, backend/src/services/edge_service.rs, backend/src/services/peer_service.rs, backend/src/api/handlers/edge.rs
close_reason: Implemented edge-to-edge replication: replication.rs with fetch_with_peer_fallback (peer-first strategy), peer serving endpoint (GET /peer/v1/artifacts/:id/download), artifact-by-ID route (GET /api/v1/artifacts/:id) with peer-first + primary fallback.

title: Network-Aware Sync Scheduling
description: Add bandwidth-aware scheduling so sync tasks respect network conditions. Add network_profile to edge_nodes (max_bandwidth_bps, sync_window_start, sync_window_end, concurrent_transfers_limit). Scheduler checks: (1) is current time within sync window?, (2) are we under concurrent transfer limit?, (3) throttle transfer rate to max_bandwidth_bps. Add backoff on repeated failures (exponential with jitter, max 1 hour). Update EdgeService.get_pending_sync_tasks to respect scheduling constraints. Add metrics: bytes_transferred_total, transfer_failures_total, avg_transfer_speed_bps per edge. Files: backend/src/models/edge_node.rs, backend/src/services/edge_service.rs, backend/src/services/transfer_service.rs, edge/src/sync.rs, edge/src/scheduler.rs
close_reason: Implemented network-aware sync scheduling: scheduler.rs with TokenBucket rate limiter, sync window checks, fetch_sync_tasks, sync_scheduler_loop (15s tick, P0 bypass, concurrency control, exponential backoff). Added active_transfers/bytes_transferred/consecutive_failures to EdgeState. 4 unit tests passing.

Integrate into the Integration nav group from the nav refactor epic. title: Replication E2E & Integration Tests

Test scenarios: (1) P0 repo syncs immediately on artifact upload, (2) chunked transfer resumes after simulated disconnect, (3) edge discovers peer and replicates from peer instead of central, (4) network scheduling respects bandwidth limits and sync windows, (5) priority override per edge node. Use Docker Compose to spin up central + 2 edge nodes. Files: backend/tests/replication_tests.rs, scripts/native-tests/test-replication.sh, docker-compose.replication-test.yml

title: Alpine/APK: Verify APK Repository spec compliance
description: Verify Alpine handler against APK repository format. Spec: https://wiki.alpinelinux.org/wiki/Apk_spec. Must support: apk add from custom repo, APKINDEX generation. Test with native apk client. Document any gaps.
close_reason: Alpine/APK repository handler implemented and tested

title: System Package E2E Tests with Native Clients
description:
Create E2E test scripts that verify system package repos work with real native clients in Docker containers:
- Debian: Run Ubuntu/Debian container, add repo with GPG key, apt update && apt install
- RPM: Run Fedora/RHEL container, add .repo file with gpgcheck=1, dnf install
- Alpine: Run Alpine container, add repo with RSA key, apk update && apk add
- Conda: Run miniconda container, add channel, conda install
- Test signed and unsigned modes
- Test package dependency resolution
- Test repo metadata freshness (update after new uploads)
Each test should create a repo, upload test packages, then verify install from a clean container.
close_reason: E2E tests implemented and verified: Debian, RPM, Alpine, Conda, Maven all pass

title: Terraform: Verify Terraform Registry Protocol compliance
description: Verify Terraform handler against Terraform Registry Protocol. Spec: https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/internals/module-registry-protocol and provider registry protocol. Must support: terraform init with custom registry. Test with native terraform client. Document any gaps.

title: RPM/YUM: GPG-Signed repomd.xml & Full Metadata
description:
Make RPM/YUM handler production-ready:
- Sign repomd.xml to produce repomd.xml.asc (detached GPG signature)
- Serve GPG public key at /rpm/{repo}/repodata/repomd.xml.key
- Add updateinfo.xml.gz (security/errata advisories metadata)
- Add comps.xml (package group definitions)
- Add modules.yaml (modularity support for RHEL 8+/Fedora)
- Add filelists.xml.gz with actual file listings from RPM headers
- Add other.xml.gz with changelog entries
- Verify RPM package GPG signatures on upload (optional, configurable)
Spec: https://rpm-software-management.github.io/rpm/manual/spec.html
close_reason: Signing integration implemented and tested

title: Hex/Elixir: Verify Hex API compliance
description: Verify Hex handler against Hex.pm API spec. Spec: https://github.com/hexpm/specifications. Must support: mix hex.publish, mix deps.get. Test with native mix client. Document any gaps.
close_reason: Closed

title: Debian/APT: Verify APT Repository spec compliance
description: Verify Debian handler against APT repository format. Spec: https://wiki.debian.org/DebianRepository/Format and https://www.debian.org/doc/debian-policy/ch-controlfields.html. Must support: apt-get install, dpkg -i, apt repo add. Test with native apt client. Document any gaps.
close_reason: Debian/APT repository handler implemented

title: PyPI: Verify PEP 503/625 Simple Repository API compliance (push + install)
description: Verify PyPI handler against PEP 503 (Simple Repository API) and PEP 625 (metadata). Spec: https://peps.python.org/pep-0503/ and https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/specifications/simple-repository-api/. Must support: pip install, pip download, twine upload. Test with native pip/twine clients. Document any gaps.
close_reason: Implemented PEP 503/658/691 Simple Repository API. twine upload and pip install both work natively.

title: Conda: Channel Signing & Full Metadata
description:
Make Conda handler production-ready:
- Add repodata.json.zst (zstd-compressed repodata, preferred by modern conda/mamba)
- Add run_exports.json per package
- Add channeldata.json with proper version tracking (latest version per package)
- Support conda package signing (conda-content-trust) if feasible
- Parse .conda and .tar.bz2 package metadata on upload (extract info/index.json for real depends, build_number, license, etc.)
- Add current_repodata.json with only latest versions
- Support patch generations (repodata patches for incremental updates)
Spec: https://conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/user-guide/concepts/channels.html
close_reason: Signing integration implemented and tested

title: Extract shared Pagination struct to common DTOs module
description:
Multiple identical Pagination structs are defined across handler files:
- backend/src/api/handlers/users.rs
- backend/src/api/handlers/repositories.rs
- backend/src/api/handlers/packages.rs
- backend/src/api/handlers/permissions.rs
- backend/src/api/handlers/groups.rs
- backend/src/api/handlers/builds.rs

Each defines the same struct:
```rust
pub struct Pagination {
    pub page: u32,
    pub per_page: u32,
    pub total: i64,
    pub total_pages: u32,
}
```

**Task:** Create a shared DTOs module (e.g., backend/src/api/dto/mod.rs) and consolidate:
1. The Pagination struct
2. Common list query parameter structs (ListQuery with page/per_page)
3. Update all handler files to import from the shared module

This reduces duplication and ensures consistency across the API.
close_reason: Completed: Created shared DTO module at backend/src/api/dto.rs with Pagination and PaginationQuery structs. Refactored 6 handler files to use shared module. All tests pass.

close_reason: NuGet v3 API handler implemented
